concepts.label: Atom
concepts.definition: The smallest unit of matter that retains the properties of an element, consisting of a nucleus surrounded by electrons.

concepts.label: Molecule
concepts.definition: A group of two or more atoms held together by chemical bonds that acts as a single unit.

concepts.label: Element
concepts.definition: A pure substance consisting of only one type of atom, characterized by its atomic number.

concepts.label: Compound
concepts.definition: A substance formed when two or more different elements are chemically bonded together in fixed proportions.

concepts.label: Chemical Reaction
concepts.definition: A process in which one or more substances are converted into different substances through the breaking and forming of chemical bonds.

concepts.label: Catalyst
concepts.definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.

concepts.label: pH
concepts.definition: A scale from 0 to 14 that measures the acidity or alkalinity of a solution, with 7 being neutral.

concepts.label: Acid
concepts.definition: A substance that donates hydrogen ions (H+) in aqueous solution, typically having a pH less than 7.

concepts.label: Base
concepts.definition: A substance that accepts hydrogen ions (H+) or donates hydroxide ions (OH-) in aqueous solution, typically having a pH greater than 7.

concepts.label: Oxidation
concepts.definition: A chemical process in which an atom, ion, or molecule loses electrons, often involving the addition of oxygen or removal of hydrogen.

concepts.label: Reduction
concepts.definition: A chemical process in which an atom, ion, or molecule gains electrons, often involving the removal of oxygen or addition of hydrogen.

concepts.label: Covalent Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by the sharing of electron pairs between atoms, typically between non-metal atoms.

concepts.label: Ionic Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions, typically between a metal and non-metal.

concepts.label: Electron Shell
concepts.definition: An energy level around an atomic nucleus where electrons are found, with shells filling in order of increasing energy.

concepts.label: Periodic Table
concepts.definition: A systematic arrangement of chemical elements in order of increasing atomic number, showing recurring patterns of properties.

concepts.label: Mole
concepts.definition: The SI unit for amount of substance, equal to approximately 6.022 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's number).

concepts.label: Solution
concepts.definition: A homogeneous mixture composed of a solute dissolved in a solvent, forming a single phase.

concepts.label: Solvent
concepts.definition: The substance in a solution that dissolves the solute, typically present in the greater amount.

concepts.label: Polymer
concepts.definition: A large molecule composed of many repeated smaller units (monomers) connected by covalent bonds.

concepts.label: Isotope
concepts.definition: Variants of the same element that have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons in their nuclei.

concepts.label: Alkane
concepts.definition: Saturated hydrocarbons containing only single covalent bonds between carbon atoms, with the general formula CnH2n+2.

concepts.label: Alkene
concepts.definition: Unsaturated hydrocarbons containing at least one carbon-carbon double bond, with the general formula CnH2n.

concepts.label: Alcohol
concepts.definition: Organic compounds containing one or more hydroxyl (-OH) functional groups attached to carbon atoms.

concepts.label: Aldehyde
concepts.definition: Organic compounds containing a carbonyl group (C=O) with the carbon atom bonded to at least one hydrogen atom.

concepts.label: Ketone
concepts.definition: Organic compounds containing a carbonyl group (C=O) bonded to two carbon atoms, with no hydrogen on the carbonyl carbon.

concepts.label: Ester
concepts.definition: Organic compounds formed by the reaction between a carboxylic acid and an alcohol, characterized by the -COO- functional group.

concepts.label: Amine
concepts.definition: Organic compounds derived from ammonia (NH3) where one or more hydrogen atoms are replaced by organic groups.

concepts.label: Benzene
concepts.definition: A aromatic hydrocarbon (C6H6) with a ring of six carbon atoms connected by alternating single and double bonds.

concepts.label: Carboxylic Acid
concepts.definition: Organic compounds containing the carboxyl functional group (-COOH), characterized by acidic properties.

concepts.label: Amino Acid
concepts.definition: Organic compounds containing both amino (-NH2) and carboxyl (-COOH) functional groups, the building blocks of proteins.

concepts.label: Lipid
concepts.definition: A diverse group of hydrophobic biomolecules including fats, oils, and phospholipids that serve as energy storage and membrane components.

concepts.label: Carbohydrate
concepts.definition: Biomolecules composed of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen, typically in a 1:2:1 ratio, serving as energy sources and structural components.

concepts.label: ATP
concepts.definition: Adenosine triphosphate, the primary energy currency of cells, storing and releasing energy through phosphate bond hydrolysis.

concepts.label: Glucose
concepts.definition: A simple sugar (C6H12O6) that serves as the primary source of energy for cellular metabolism in most organisms.

concepts.label: Electrolysis
concepts.definition: A process that uses electrical current to drive a non-spontaneous chemical reaction, decomposing compounds into their elements.

concepts.label: Battery
concepts.definition: An electrochemical device that converts stored chemical energy into electrical energy through spontaneous redox reactions.

concepts.label: Galvanic Cell
concepts.definition: An electrochemical cell that generates electrical energy from spontaneous redox reactions, also known as a voltaic cell.

concepts.label: Electrode
concepts.definition: A conductor through which electric current enters or leaves an electrochemical cell, classified as anode or cathode.

concepts.label: Noble Gas
concepts.definition: Chemical elements in Group 18 of the periodic table that are generally unreactive due to their complete valence electron shells.

concepts.label: Transition Metal
concepts.definition: Elements in the d-block of the periodic table characterized by partially filled d orbitals and variable oxidation states.

concepts.label: Alloy
concepts.definition: A metallic material composed of two or more elements, typically metals, combined to enhance specific properties.

concepts.label: Ceramic
concepts.definition: Inorganic, non-metallic materials typically made from clay and hardened by heat, known for their hardness and heat resistance.

concepts.label: Crystal Lattice
concepts.definition: A three-dimensional arrangement of atoms, ions, or molecules in a crystalline solid, forming a repeating pattern.

concepts.label: Enthalpy
concepts.definition: A thermodynamic property representing the total heat content of a system at constant pressure, symbol H.

concepts.label: Exothermic
concepts.definition: Chemical reactions that release heat energy to the surroundings, resulting in a negative change in enthalpy.

concepts.label: Endothermic
concepts.definition: Chemical reactions that absorb heat energy from the surroundings, resulting in a positive change in enthalpy.

concepts.label: Activation Energy
concepts.definition: The minimum energy required for reactants to overcome the energy barrier and proceed to form products in a chemical reaction.

concepts.label: Gibbs Free Energy
concepts.definition: A thermodynamic potential that determines the spontaneity of a process at constant temperature and pressure, symbol G.

concepts.label: Hydrocarbon
concepts.definition: Organic compounds composed entirely of hydrogen and carbon atoms, forming the basis of fossil fuels and many organic molecules.

concepts.label: Functional Group
concepts.definition: Specific arrangements of atoms in organic molecules that determine the chemical properties and reactions of the compound.

concepts.label: Enzyme
concepts.definition: Biological catalysts that accelerate biochemical reactions by lowering activation energy, typically proteins with specific active sites.

concepts.label: Protein
concepts.definition: Large biomolecules composed of amino acid chains that fold into specific three-dimensional structures to perform cellular functions.

concepts.label: Metabolism
concepts.definition: The sum of all chemical reactions occurring in living organisms to maintain life, including catabolism and anabolism.

concepts.label: Ion
concepts.definition: An atom or molecule that has gained or lost electrons, resulting in a net positive (cation) or negative (anion) charge.

concepts.label: Salt
concepts.definition: An ionic compound formed by the neutralization reaction between an acid and a base, composed of cations and anions.

concepts.label: Metal
concepts.definition: Elements characterized by high electrical and thermal conductivity, malleability, and tendency to lose electrons in chemical reactions.

concepts.label: Nonmetal
concepts.definition: Elements that typically gain electrons in chemical reactions and have properties opposite to metals, including poor conductivity.

concepts.label: Electronegativity
concepts.definition: A measure of an atom's ability to attract electrons in a chemical bond, influencing bond polarity and type.

concepts.label: Bond Energy
concepts.definition: The amount of energy required to break a specific chemical bond or the energy released when that bond forms.

concepts.label: Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: The branch of chemistry that deals with energy changes and the relationship between heat, work, and chemical reactions.
